message:
chore(beads): close 9itc (RESP3 Map, partial)

HGETALL + CONFIG GET migrated; remaining commands tracked under
frankenredis-f6z6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"frankenredis-9itc","title":"fr-command: RESP3 map-typed replies emit as Array instead of Map (HGETALL, CONFIG GET, XINFO STREAM, CLUSTER SHARDS, CLIENT INFO, etc.)","description":"Multiple commands return map-typed replies in RESP3 mode upstream (Redis 7.2 networking.c::addReplyMap*) but fr's command implementations always emit RespFrame::Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version. Affected commands include:

  - HGETALL h
  - CONFIG GET <param> (returns key/value map)
  - HRANDFIELD h N WITHVALUES
  - CLIENT INFO (single client info as RESP3 map)
  - XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS

When redis-cli connects with -3, fr replies with array form (display: 'k\nv\nk\nv\n...'); upstream replies with map form (display: 'k v\nk v\n...').

Acceptance: when session.resp_protocol_version == 3, the listed commands emit RespFrame::Map(Some(pairs)) where each pair is (key, value). For fr-command this means routing the protocol_version through the dispatch path or providing a render-time override. fr-protocol already has the Map variant (crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:13).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) with -3 flag.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T05:23:37.748476834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T05:23:37.748476834Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id": "frankenredis-9itc", "title": "fr-command: RESP3 map-typed replies emit as Array instead of Map (HGETALL, CONFIG GET, XINFO STREAM, CLUSTER SHARDS, CLIENT INFO, etc.)", "description": "Multiple commands return map-typed replies in RESP3 mode upstream (Redis 7.2 networking.c::addReplyMap*) but fr's command implementations always emit RespFrame::Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version. Affected commands include:

  - HGETALL h
  - CONFIG GET <param> (returns key/value map)
  - HRANDFIELD h N WITHVALUES
  - CLIENT INFO (single client info as RESP3 map)
  - XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS

When redis-cli connects with -3, fr replies with array form (display: 'k\nv\nk\nv\n...'); upstream replies with map form (display: 'k v\nk v\n...').

Acceptance: when session.resp_protocol_version == 3, the listed commands emit RespFrame::Map(Some(pairs)) where each pair is (key, value). For fr-command this means routing the protocol_version through the dispatch path or providing a render-time override. fr-protocol already has the Map variant (crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:13).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) with -3 flag.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:23:37.748476834Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T18:19:31.572911Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T18:19:31.572911Z", "close_reason": "HGETALL and CONFIG GET migrated to emit RespFrame::Map in RESP3 mode (ad8462a, a655529). XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS, HRANDFIELD WITHVALUES tracked separately under frankenredis-f6z6."}
 {"id": "frankenredis-9jl4", "title": "v8s5 part a: document upstream DUMP/RESTORE payload format (fr-store reference)", "description": "Preparation sub-bead for br-frankenredis-v8s5. Extract the upstream DUMP payload layout from legacy_redis_code/redis/src/cluster.c::dumpCommand + rdb.c::rdbSaveObject + restoreCommand + rdb.c::rdbLoadObject and write it up as crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md.

Scope:
  * Overall payload: type_byte + rdbSaveObject + version (u16 LE) + CRC64 (u64 LE).
  * Version field semantics (currently upstream is version 11 for 7.2.x, 12 for 7.4.x).
  * Per-type rdbSaveObject layout for STRING/LIST_QUICKLIST_2/SET_INTSET/SET_LISTPACK/HASH_LISTPACK/HASH_METADATA/ZSET_LISTPACK/STREAM_LISTPACKS_3.
  * CRC64 polynomial (Redis CRC64 with the Jones polynomial, already implemented at crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs:848).
  * A comparison table: current frankenredis-internal layout vs upstream expectation, field-by-field.

No code changes.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:17:59.107620059Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T16:24:22.963409003Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T16:24:22.961489188Z", "close_reason": "Reference doc crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md already exists (260 lines) covering envelope/footer, RDB length + string encodings, all upstream type tags (0/2/4/5/11/16/17/18/20/21 + Redis 7.4 24/25), per-type body layouts, fr-store delta vs upstream, and decoder/encoder checklist. No further work needed for the docs-only sub-bead.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-f6z6","title":"fr-command: XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS + CLIENT INFO + HRANDFIELD WITHVALUES still emit Array instead of RESP3 Map","description":"Follow-up to br-frankenredis-9itc. HGETALL and CONFIG GET were migrated to emit RespFrame::Map for RESP3 callers (commits ad8462a, a655529). The remaining map-typed reply commands still always emit Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version:

  - XINFO STREAM (and FULL): flat alternating-k/v at fr-command/src/lib.rs:6182
  - XINFO GROUPS: per-group flat-array elements
  - XINFO CONSUMERS: per-consumer flat-array elements
  - HRANDFIELD key N WITHVALUES: alternating-k/v
  - CLIENT INFO: single-client info has historically been a bulk-string with newlines (not actually a map, even in RESP3) — leave alone unless verified.

Acceptance: each of the listed commands emits RespFrame::Map(Some(...)) in RESP3 mode, matching upstream addReplyMap*","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:19:20.513867431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:19:20.513867431Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-fap1", "title": "Document threat model per subsystem as required by Security Doctrine", "description": "# Threat Model Documentation

## What
AGENTS.md Security Doctrine requires "Threat model notes for each major subsystem." Create a THREAT_MODEL.md documenting:

## Subsystems to Cover
1. **Network/RESP**: Malformed frames, oversized bulk strings, slowloris attacks, pipeline flooding
2. **Authentication**: Brute force AUTH, timing attacks on password comparison
3. **Persistence**: RDB corruption, AOF truncation, file permission attacks
4. **Replication**: Rogue replica injection, PSYNC replay, snapshot poisoning
5. **Lua scripting**: Infinite loops (existing time limit), memory exhaustion, redis.call abuse
6. **Commands**: Integer overflow in INCR/INCRBY, memory exhaustion via APPEND/LPUSH, key enumeration timing

## Implementation
Create docs/THREAT_MODEL.md with:
- Per-subsystem threat inventory
- Current mitigations (what we do)
- Residual risks (what we dont cover)
- Priority of remaining mitigations

## Files
- docs/THREAT_MODEL.md (new)", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:50.329019047Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:14:05.413683101Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:14:05.413405296Z", "close_reason": "Created docs/THREAT_MODEL.md covering 6 subsystems: Network/RESP, Authentication, Persistence, Replication, Lua Scripting, Command-Level. Each subsystem has threat inventory, current mitigations (with code references), and residual risks. Prioritized remaining mitigations.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-faqe", "title": "[HIGH] ACL parity: 31 divergences — AUTH default-user password flow, ACL CAT wording + order, ACL DRYRUN reply shape, ACL SAVE/LOAD config-file awareness, ACL LOG entries", "description": "Surfaced by live_redis_core_acl_matches_runtime (31 cases): (a) AUTH rejects SETUSER-configured passwords because fr-runtime's default ACL state is nopass-locked; every auth_* + acl_*_auth_* + acl_*_reauth_* case sees 'ERR AUTH <password> called without any password configured' instead of matching upstream. (b) ACL LIST default-user rule order: ours emits 'user default on sanitize-payload nopass ~* &* +@all' vs upstream 'user default on nopass sanitize-payload ~* &* +@all' — different flag ordering. (c) ACL CAT unknown-category wording: ours 'ERR Unknown ACL cat category' vs upstream 'ERR Unknown category'. (d) ACL CAT <category> entry order: our command-to-category mapping iterates in registration order, upstream in dict order. (e) ACL SAVE/LOAD returns OK where upstream returns 'ERR This Redis instance is not configured to use an ACL file' (our harness spawns without an ACL file too but fr-runtime still pretends SAVE succeeded). (f) ACL DRYRUN returns Error where upstream returns BulkString with the denial message. (g) ACL LOG is empty (we don't record failed auth attempts) where upstream has 3 entries. (h) ACL GENPASS extra-args emits 'wrong number of arguments for acl|genpass' vs upstream 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for GENPASS'. Fix in fr-runtime + fr-command ACL paths.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T13:14:56.156122998Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T15:56:29.098170824Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T15:56:29.096354788Z", "close_reason": "Resolved across 7 PRs: ACL CAT category membership + order canonicalizer, DRYRUN BulkString reply + wording, SAVE/LOAD upstream 'not configured' wording, atomic SETUSER rollback, AUTH syntax-error arity, session-isolated dedicated-connection that shares ACL subsystem, ACL LOG volatile-field canonicalizer. live_redis_core_acl now 130/130 under STRICT mode.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-oe10", "title": "Runtime does not yet consume Lua script propagation masks", "description": "Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs and crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs now record per-command Lua propagation masks for redis.set_repl(REPL_NONE/REPL_AOF/REPL_REPLICA/REPL_ALL), but fr-runtime still records top-level EVAL/FCALL writes without consulting that metadata.

Gap: selective script propagation is now modeled in command/store state, but runtime backlog/AOF capture still needs to consume those records so replica backlog and script-side propagation control diverge less from Redis.

Real impl path: thread store.script_propagation_records through the runtime EVAL/FCALL capture path in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs and decide how to route AOF-vs-replica destinations against the existing persistence/backlog model.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-22T13:22:29.484498154Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T13:50:10Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T13:50:10Z", "close_reason": "Runtime now consumes Lua script propagation records", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-omff", "title": "rv89 part 4a: OBJECT ENCODING listpack_ex/hashtable_ex + keyspace events + DEBUG OBJECT expired_fields", "description": "Narrowed slice of br-frankenredis-3jrv. No RDB / AOF / HGETEX-HGETDEL yet — those are part 4b (RDB) and part 4c (AOF + HGETEX/HGETDEL).

Scope:
- fr-store::object_encoding: when a hash carries at least one per-field TTL, report 'listpack_ex' / 'hashtable_ex' instead of 'listpack' / 'hashtable'.
- Keyspace events: emit 'hexpire', 'hpexpire', 'hexpireat', 'hpexpireat', 'hpersist', 'hexpired' on the right commands / lazy-reaps.
- DEBUG OBJECT reports an 'expired_fields' counter equal to the Store's stat_expired_keys-derived delta for the given key.
- Tests per axis.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-23T23:14:51.577273514Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T23:17:08.712977320Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-23T23:17:08.711013313Z", "close_reason": "Landed in f462d78: OBJECT ENCODING flips listpack/hashtable → listpack_ex/hashtable_ex when any per-field TTL is present; hexpired keyspace event fires on both bulk and targeted lazy reaps. 2 new tests; fr-store 280/0. Command-side keyspace events (HEXPIRE → hexpire etc.) and DEBUG OBJECT expired_fields deferred to a future bead alongside RDB + AOF (3jrv remains open).", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
